title: tradescantia multibracteata
intro: Tradescantia multibracteata, also known as Wandering Willie or Spiderwings, is a low-growing, spreading plant native to Mexico and Central America. Tradescantia multibracteata is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and is sometimes used in herbal preparations to treat skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Tradescantia multibracteata can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and the plant is also sometimes used in topical creams and ointments.
